description:
Advances: specled.coverage_capture

Deliverable:
SpecLedEx.Coverage.Formatter (ExUnit formatter) with snapshot_fn DI (default &:cover.analyse/1) and anonymous ETS (:ets.new(:anon, [:public, :set])); per-test state keyed by test_pid carrying {snapshot, tags, test_id}; SpecLedEx.Coverage.Store (reader + build_records/1 helper) writes/reads .spec/_coverage/per_test.coverdata as ETF; split Coverage.init/2 (pure config) vs Coverage.install/1 (impure); mix spec.cover.test task forces async: false globally via Application.put_env(:ex_unit, :async, false) + ExUnit.configure(async: false) before loading test modules, wraps mix test --cover, logs async: true test files on exit; SpecLedEx.IntegrationCase with run_fixture_mix_test(root, args) using System.cmd for child BEAM.

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - mix test test/specled_ex/coverage/formatter_test.exs
  - mix test test/specled_ex/coverage/store_test.exs
  - mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs --include integration
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - specled.coverage_capture.scenario.formatter_stub_snapshot
  - specled.coverage_capture.scenario.store_round_trip
  - specled.coverage_capture.scenario.spec_cover_test_forces_serial

Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/formatter.ex
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/store.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.test.ex
- test/specled_ex/coverage/formatter_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/coverage/store_test.exs
- test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs
- test_support/specled_ex_integration_case.ex (extend if needed — S2 stub)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex (S4b)
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex (S4b)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/**
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/**
- test/integration/scenario_* (S4b owns those tests)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT attempt async-safe per-test attribution; :cover has no per-pid surface (decision: specled.decision.serialized_per_test_coverage).
- Do NOT use named ETS tables; parallel formatter instances in unit tests must coexist.
- Do NOT add duration_ms / captured_at timestamps (scope cut #23) or schema version fields (cut #26).
- Do NOT ship an adapter/ directory for ExCoveralls (scope cut #18); if adapter ships here, flat file at lib/specled_ex/coverage/excoveralls.ex (otherwise defer to v1.x).
- mix test --cover must continue to work unchanged in its cumulative mode.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] mix spec.cover.test produces .spec/_coverage/per_test.coverdata with at least one record per test on the fixture
- [ ] No use of named ETS in coverage modules (grep check)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Files touched:

- test_support/specled_ex_integration_case.ex (added run_fixture_mix_test/2)
- test/specled_ex/coverage/formatter_test.exs (new — 6 tests)
- test/specled_ex/coverage/store_test.exs (new — 7 tests)
- test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs (new — 1 integration test)
- .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md (verification stubs flipped to execute: true)

Tests added (per scenario):
- formatter_stub_snapshot: SpecLedEx.Coverage.FormatterTest "calls snapshot_fn exactly once with the cover module set and stores the record under test_pid"
- store_round_trip: SpecLedEx.Coverage.StoreTest "write then read returns input records byte-equal, order preserved"
- spec_cover_test_forces_serial: Mix.Tasks.Spec.Cover.TestTest "runs serialized, warns about async: true files, writes per_test.coverdata"

Plus supporting tests for init/install split, anonymous-ETS isolation, fallback keying, build_records normalization, mkdir_p on write.

Verification stubs flipped:
- mix test test/specled_ex/coverage/formatter_test.exs -> execute: true (passes)
- mix test test/specled_ex/coverage/store_test.exs -> execute: true (passes)
- mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs --include integration -> execute: true (passes)

Summary:
Implements per-test coverage capture per the serialized_per_test_coverage ADR.
Coverage.init/2 (pure config) is split from install/1 (allocates anonymous ETS),
making the formatter testable without :cover or globals. The formatter keys
per-test state by tags[:test_pid] when present, falling back to {module, name}
since ExUnit does not propagate the test process pid into test.tags (only into
context). The on-disk record's test_pid field falls back to the formatter's pid
when tags omit it; the field is always a pid as required.

mix spec.cover.test sets Application.put_env(:ex_unit, :async, false) and
ExUnit.configure(async: false) before delegating to mix test --cover, then
emits a stderr warning naming any test files that opt back in to async: true
(per spec, that is a user bug — task does not silently serialize them).

The integration test scaffolds a tmp project with one async: true and one
async: false test module; the fixture mix.exs reads SPECLED_EX_EBIN (set by
run_fixture_mix_test/2 alongside ERL_LIBS) and Code.append_path's it so
parent-defined Mix tasks resolve in the child BEAM. mix test --cover keeps
working unchanged in cumulative mode.

No named ETS, no async-safe attribution attempt, no duration_ms / captured_at
/ schema_version fields, no adapter directory — all per Out of Scope (intent).

Project verification: mix test --include integration -> 327 tests, 0 failures.
mix spec.check -> 0 findings on specled.coverage_capture (14 pre-existing
warnings, all on other subjects: expanded_behavior_tier, triangulation,
use_tier — all S4b/S5a work).

  * formatter_snapshot_fn_di: formatter_test "calls snapshot_fn exactly once" + refute_received guard; formatter source routes via state.snapshot_fn (no direct :cover.analyse call)
  * keyed_by_test_pid: formatter_test asserts :ets.lookup(table, pid) returns {pid, {stub_return, tags, test_id}} with the test_id string built from module.name
  * anonymous_ets: formatter_test asserts :ets.info(table, :named_table) == false AND two installs return distinct tables; only :ets.new call in coverage modules is :ets.new(:anon, [:public, :set])
  * artifact_path: store_test asserts default_path == ".spec/_coverage/per_test.coverdata"; round-trip test confirms ETF encoding of all 5 fields (test_id, file, lines_hit, tags, test_pid)
  * store_split: Store is a separate module; build_records/1 has 3 dedicated tests (normalization, order preservation, KeyError on missing field)

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Implementer's framing matches the diff. The Coverage.init/install split is genuine: init/2 is pure (no ETS, no :cover), install/1 allocates the anonymous table — formatter_test verifies this directly.
- The on-disk test_pid field falls back to self() when tags omit :test_pid — documented in the formatter moduledoc and consistent with the spec wording (the field is always a pid). The ETS key has its own fallback to {module, name} (also tested).
- The integration fixture's mix.exs reads SPECLED_EX_EBIN (set by run_fixture_mix_test/2) and Code.append_path's it so the parent's spec.cover.test resolves in the child BEAM. Integration test passes in 0.4s.
